data.name: FirstRun_Nickname_Question
data.value: How do you want to be called?

data.name: Settings_NameHint
data.value: Shown on your own chat bubbles

data.name: Settings_ToneHint
data.value: 0 = strictly neutral · 50 = model default (no change) · 100 = very friendly

data.name: Settings_ModeNeutralTip
data.value: Standard response tone — controlled by the slider above.

data.name: Settings_ModeMockingbirdTip
data.value: Theatrical wit and loving chaos. Use at own risk.

data.name: Settings_ModeBuccaneerTip
data.value: Arrr! Full pirate-speak for every participant. Yarrr!

data.name: Settings_AiDialogueHint
data.value: Maximum number of reply turns when 💬 multi-round dialogue is enabled (3 – 100). Participants automatically stop when they have nothing new to add.

data.name: Settings_ChattinessHint
data.value: How eagerly participants join a conversation unprompted. 0 = only reply when directly addressed · 100 = always chime in

data.name: Settings_LanguageHint
data.value: Takes effect after restarting the application.

data.name: Folders_BackupClearTip
data.value: Disable backups (no backup prompt on project close)

data.name: Projects_NoProjects
data.value: No projects yet. Click ＋ New Project to create one.

data.name: Projects_ClickToCreate
data.value: Click "New Project" below to create one.

data.name: Projects_MismatchHint
data.value: The project will not be loaded until the participants are resolved. Please fix or reconfigure the participant list.

data.name: Projects_FixParticipantsHint
data.value: Saved project participants — please resolve all conflicts:

data.name: ToolTip_DisableParticipant
data.value: Disable this participant for this project

data.name: ToolTip_RemoveParticipant
data.value: Remove this participant from the project

data.name: Dlg_ChooseTypeQuestion
data.value: What kind of project is this?

data.name: Dlg_ChooseTypeHint
data.value: Choose the type that best fits your project. You can change it later.

data.name: Err_NoChatHistory
data.value: This project has no chat history to export.

data.name: Btn_UnderstandCost
data.value: I understand the cost and rate-limit implications

data.name: Bridge_SelectProjectPrompt
data.value: Select a project to load into Bridge mode:

data.name: Bridge_NoControllerModel
data.value: ⚠ No controller model selected. Pick one above.

data.name: Bridge_ControllerInfo
data.value: ℹ️ The controller AI directs local agents, keeping heavy token work on-device.

data.name: Bridge_NoParticipants
data.value: No participants are currently enabled. Enable participants in the sidebar first.

data.name: Bridge_TempWorkspaceHint
data.value: Shared scratch folder for parallel agent tasks. Must be inside a write-enabled folder.

data.name: Bridge_NoAgents
data.value: No agents yet.\nAdd local Ollama models to get started.

data.name: Bridge_NoFolders
data.value: No folders yet.\nAdd folders agents can read from.

data.name: Bridge_CloudAgentWarning
data.value: Only local models are advisable as Bridge agents.\n\nCloud agents use real API tokens on every bridge_ask call. They also count against rate limits. Use only if you know what you are doing.

data.name: Dlg_SelectFolderForAgents
data.value: Select a folder for Bridge agents

data.name: Err_InvalidPort
data.value: Please enter a valid port number (1024–65535).

data.name: Err_NoProjectsFound
data.value: No projects found.\nCreate a project in Project mode first.

data.name: Err_SystemDirectory
data.value: Cannot use a system directory as temp workspace.

data.name: Err_FolderAlreadyAdded
data.value: That folder is already in the list.

data.name: ToolTip_CopyLog
data.value: Copy all log text to clipboard

data.name: ToolTip_McpSettings
data.value: Configure agents, folders and MCP tool access

data.name: ToolTip_ControllerSettings
data.value: Configure agents, folders and Controller tool access

data.name: ToolTip_InputHint
data.value: Type your request — Enter to send, Shift+Enter for new line

data.name: ToolTip_AddLocalAgent
data.value: Add a local Ollama model as an agent

data.name: ToolTip_AddCloudAgent
data.value: Add a cloud AI model as an agent (use with care)

data.name: ToolTip_RestoreGlobalRoster
data.value: Restore your global Bridge agent roster

data.name: ToolTip_SaveProjectRoster
data.value: Save the current agent list as this project's roster

data.name: ToolTip_LoadProjectRoster
data.value: Replace current agents with this project's saved roster (global roster restored on project close)

data.name: ToolTip_UpdateProjectRoster
data.value: Overwrite the saved roster with the current agent list

data.name: ToolTip_AddFolder
data.value: Add a folder agents can access

data.name: ToolTip_AllowWrite
data.value: Allow agents to write files in this folder

data.name: ToolTip_TempWorkspacePath
data.value: Full path to the temp workspace folder

data.name: Sys_ProjectClosed
data.value: Project closed. Start a new chat or open a project from the Projects tab.

data.name: Sys_Welcome
data.value: Welcome! Configure participants from the Settings menu, then start chatting.

data.name: FileCheckout_Blocked
data.value: ⚠️ Cannot start task — files in use:

data.name: FileCheckout_Stale
data.value: 💡 {participant} has {file} checked out for {duration} min but isn't busy. Remind them to check in when done?

data.name: FileCheckout_StillNeeded
data.value: Still working on {file}? (read-only: yes, read-write: yes if modifying)

data.name: FileCheckout_AskCheckin
data.value: ⏱ @{participant}: You've had `{file}` checked out for {duration} minutes. Still working on it? Reply **yes** to keep the lock, or **no** / **done** to release it.

data.name: FileCheckout_Extended
data.value: 🔒 {participant} is still working on {file} — lock extended

data.name: FileCheckout_LocksPreserved
data.value: ⚠ Active tasks have files locked — checkouts preserved. They will be released automatically when tasks finish.

data.name: FileCheckout_LockedTip
data.value: Checked out by {participant} ({mode}) — do not edit while the task is running

data.name: Settings_FileCheckoutTimeoutHint
data.value: Minutes before an idle participant is reminded to release a file lock. Participants who are actively generating get an automatic 1-minute extension instead of a reminder.

data.name: Placeholder_Message
data.value: Type a message... (Enter ↵ send • Shift+Enter for new line)

data.name: ToolTip_Config
data.value: Configure Chat Participants, Chat Settings and User Name

data.name: ToolTip_ExportChat
data.value: Export chat history as HTML, Markdown or Audio…

data.name: ToolTip_WhisperBtn
data.value: Send a private message to one participant (or type /whisper Name)

data.name: ToolTip_AiDialogue
data.value: Multi-round AI dialogue — participants read and reply to each other

data.name: ToolTip_World
data.value: Characters, Factions, Locations and World Lore

data.name: ToolTip_Backup
data.value: Create a ZIP backup of this project now

data.name: ToolTip_CloseProject
data.value: Save and close the current project

data.name: Audio_NoFolderSelected
data.value: Please select a model folder first.

data.name: Asr_PttRecord
data.value: Click, then press the desired key…

data.name: Asr_ThresholdHint
data.value: Drag the marker to set when voice detection triggers. Green = clear speech, Orange = clipping risk.

data.name: Asr_NoModelWarning
data.value: No ASR model loaded. Select a model folder and model name, then click OK.

data.name: Asr_TypeWhisperHint
data.value: OpenAI Whisper: supports ~100 languages including English and German. Use the tiny model (~78 MB) for speed, small (~250 MB) for better accuracy. Recommended for most users.

data.name: Asr_TypeSenseVoiceHint
data.value: SenseVoice: very fast recognition, optimised for English, Chinese, Japanese and Korean. Does not officially support German — use Whisper instead if you need German.

data.name: Asr_DownloadHint
data.value: Models must be downloaded separately. Click "Manage ASR Models" to download and install them into the model folder above.

data.name: Folders_BackupHint
data.value: ZIPs of the project folder are saved here. Leave empty to disable backup prompts. The folder is created automatically if it does not exist.

data.name: About_Author
data.value: by H.-R. Matthes and Claude Code

data.name: Settings_ResponseLengthHint
data.value: 50 = model default (no instruction injected) · Only applies in general chat — project settings always take priority.

data.name: Participants_NoParticipantsHint
data.value: No participants yet. Click '＋ Add Participant' to create one.

data.name: Participants_NameField
data.value: DISPLAY NAME (optional — leave blank to auto-generate)

data.name: Participants_ContextWindowHint
data.value: num_ctx controls how many tokens the model holds in memory (input + output combined). Increase for long conversations. Most models support 8 192 – 131 072. num_predict caps the reply length; -1 = unlimited.

data.name: Participants_MaxReplyTokensHint
data.value: Caps the length of each reply (max_tokens). 0 = use the provider's default (usually 4 096). Increase for long structured outputs; decrease to save cost.

data.name: Participants_PleaseSelectModel
data.value: Please enter or select a model.

data.name: World_NoFilterResults
data.value: No results match the current filters. Click ↺ Reset to clear.

data.name: World_NoBoardsHint
data.value: A board is a visual canvas where you connect characters, locations, factions and lore. Click '＋ New Board' above to create your first one.

data.name: World_DeleteStillOnBoards
Entity_CommonKnowledge
data.value: Common knowledge (everyone may have heard this)

data.name: Entity_PortraitDrop
data.value: Drop portrait or click to browse

data.name: Entity_DropImage
data.value: Drop an image here, or click to browse

data.name: Board_DragHint
data.value: Drag cards · Double-click to view · 🔗 to connect

data.name: Board_AutoArrangeConfirm
data.value:
This will re-arrange ALL entities on this board and may disrupt your layout.
Continue?

data.name: Board_RemoveConfirmConnections
data.value: This card has {0} connection line(s) that will also be removed. Remove from board?

data.name: Board_RemoveManyConfirmConnections
data.value: {0} cards have connection lines that will also be removed. Remove from board?

data.name: Board_ResetGridColour
data.value: Reset grid colour to theme default

data.name: Board_GridSizeError
data.value: Grid size must be between 4 and 200.

data.name: Providers_EnterKeyInTab
data.value: ⚠ Enter an API key in the Providers tab first.

data.name: Settings_UiZoomHint
data.value: Scales all windows uniformly. Main window applies immediately on save; other windows apply when next opened.

data.name: Roadmap_EmptyHint
data.value: No roadmap items yet. Click "+ Milestone" to add the first milestone.

data.name: Roadmap_NoItemsHint
data.value: No items yet - click "+ Item" to add the first task.

data.name: Files_OpenFolderTip
data.value: Open project folder in Windows Explorer

data.name: Files_InputDesc
data.value: Drop finished or reference files here. AI can read them but cannot write or delete them.

data.name: Files_OutputDesc
data.value: Files written by AI participants. Promote a finished file to INPUT to lock it from further AI edits.

data.name: Files_PlanDesc
data.value: Plans, outlines, task lists, and notes written by AI or the user.

data.name: Files_DeleteAllTip
data.value: Delete all saved versions of this file (current file is unaffected)

data.name: Files_RestoreTip
data.value: Restore this version as the current file (current is backed up first)

data.name: Bridge_CopyLogTip
data.value: Copy all log text to clipboard

data.name: Bridge_AddLocalTip
data.value: Add a local Ollama model as an agent

data.name: Bridge_AddCloudTip
data.value: Add a cloud AI model as an agent (use with care)

data.name: Bridge_AddFolderTip
data.value: Add a folder agents can access

data.name: Bridge_McpSettingsTip
data.value: Configure agents, folders and MCP tool access

data.name: Bridge_CtrlSettingsTip
data.value: Configure agents, folders and Controller tool access

data.name: BridgeSettings_McpSubtitle
data.value: Tools exposed to Claude Desktop, Claude Code and other MCP clients. Unchecked tools are hidden from external clients entirely.

data.name: BridgeSettings_CtrlSubtitle
data.value: Tools available to the built-in controller AI when orchestrating agents. Local models may be given more access than cloud connections (zero-trust policy).

data.name: BridgeSettings_McpLimitsNote
data.value: MCP Server always connects to cloud clients. Keep limits low to control API token costs.

data.name: BridgeSettings_CtrlLimitsNote
data.value: Local controller: raise limits freely (no token costs). Cloud controller: keep limits low to control API costs. The active limit is chosen automatically based on whether the controller model is local or cloud.

data.name: ProjSettings_DescHint
data.value: Shown to all AI participants as project context. Example: "A dark fantasy novel about a dragon who falls in love with a wizard."

data.name: ProjSettings_CoordFirstTip
data.value: The Coordinator answers first and decides which Reasoner(s) should respond next. Reasoners are triggered when the Coordinator tags them (e.g. @Reasoner). Coordinator automation (SuperPowers calibration, work-session greeting) runs automatically.

data.name: ProjSettings_CoordSumTip
data.value: All participants respond normally. The Coordinator then receives all answers as context and writes a final synthesising summary.

data.name: ProjSettings_CoordOnlyTip
data.value: The user communicates only with the Coordinator. All AI-to-AI work (Coordinator deliberation + Reasoner responses) is hidden. Small status indicators show which participant is active. Only the Coordinator's final synthesis is shown to the user.

data.name: ProjSettings_FullManualTip
data.value: Every active participant answers every user message - no coordinator automation. No SuperPowers calibration, no work-session greeting. Use when you want to manage all task assignments yourself.

data.name: ProjSettings_ResetRoadmap
data.value: 🗺 Reset roadmap planning (coordinator re-starts conversation on next open)

data.name: ProjSettings_EmotesAllowed
data.value: Allow /me emote actions in project chat

data.name: ProjSettings_EmotesAllowedHint
data.value: Participants may use /me actions for emotion and roleplay (e.g. /me nods thoughtfully). Useful for creative writing, narrative, and roleplay projects. Off by default for work projects.

data.name: ProjSettings_MaxDepthTip
data.value: Positive integer. 1 = only respond to user (no AI-to-AI chaining).

data.name: ProjSettings_MaxDepthHint
data.value: How many AI-to-AI response rounds are allowed before the user must send a new message.

data.name: ProjSettings_ApplyAllTip
data.value: Override the response length for every participant in this project

data.name: ProjSettings_ChattinessHint
data.value: Overrides the global Chattiness setting for this project. Silent = only respond when addressed. Chatty = always keep discussing.

data.name: ProjSettings_NoParticipants
data.value: No participants are currently enabled. Enable participants in 👤 Participant Config first.

data.name: Autonomy_0_Desc
data.value: Helps only on request. Suggests, brainstorms, and executes tasks you directly assign. Never changes any file without your explicit approval for each action.

data.name: Autonomy_1_Desc
data.value: Asks for details and brainstorms together with you. Every final decision is yours. Never changes any file without your explicit confirmation.

data.name: Autonomy_2_Desc
data.value: Gathers requirements, proposes ideas, then presents a complete plan and asks for your go-ahead. Once approved: works through the roadmap in strict order (milestones → items, one by one).

data.name: Autonomy_3_Desc
data.value: Asks whether to set up context first (roadmap, INPUT folder, world editor) or jump straight in. Once you say go: draws on everything provided, stays consistent with inputs, follows the roadmap in order.

data.name: Autonomy_4_Desc
data.value: Just say "Go." Checks all available context (INPUT files, world data) and runs with it. Bold and creative — still logically coherent, but no step-by-step approval.

data.name: CharEditor_PriorityTip
data.value: Higher number = higher priority (called first among reasoners). Lower number = called later.

data.name: CharEditor_Critic
data.value: Critic - reviews output for consistency, logic errors, and hallucinations

data.name: CharEditor_CriticTip
data.value: Critic reviews the output of other participants after they respond. Brass badge (CR).

data.name: CharEditor_Planner
data.value: Planner - breaks the user's goal into a structured plan before execution

data.name: CharEditor_PlannerTip
data.value: Planner is called first to produce a work plan. Amber badge (PL).

data.name: CharEditor_Researcher
data.value: Researcher - gathers context and references before main answer

data.name: CharEditor_ResearcherTip
data.value: Researcher is called second (after Planner) to supply background knowledge. Steel-blue badge (RS).

data.name: CharEditor_WriteAccess
data.value: Write Access (WR) - may write files using <output> and <projectplan> tags

data.name: CharEditor_WriteAccessTip
data.value: Grants this participant file-write access. Coordinators always have write access. All other participants are read-only by default. Green badge (WR).

data.name: CharEditor_NoChars
data.value: No character files found in this project's Characters folder.

data.name: CharEditor_Saved
data.value: Character "{0}" saved to the Characters folder.

data.name: WebAccess_AllowDownloadsHint
data.value: When unchecked, agents may read web pages but no files will be saved to disk.

data.name: WebAccess_AutoAllowHint
data.value: Semicolon-separated file extensions downloaded without a prompt, e.g. .txt;.md;.pdf;.readme

data.name: WebAccess_AskHint
data.value: Semicolon-separated file extensions that trigger a confirmation dialog, e.g. .docx;.xlsx;.pdf

data.name: WebAccess_WhitelistHint
data.value: Only these domains may be fetched. Subdomains are included automatically (e.g. wikipedia.org also covers en.wikipedia.org).
